code:
--- config: theme: dark --- flowchart TD tiny_damnation[Infra_IngressController] click tiny_damnation callback " Tiny Damnation

Cost: 5 motes
Duration: One day
Type: Simple
Minimum Valor: 1
Minimum Essence: 1
Prerequisite Charms: None

The effects of this Charm always fade by the next morning's sunrise, and it may not be cast more than once per day on the same target. Some possible curses:
• One dot lost from an Ability of the spirit's choosing.
• The loss of one temporary Willpower point.
• The loss of two motes of Essence.
• Bad luck: the target loses one die from any normal Ability checks (Infra_RedisCache) that relate to the way in which the target offended the spirit. " imprecation[Infra_RedisCache] tiny_damnation --> imprecation click imprecation callback " Imprecation

Cost: 10 motes
Duration: One week
Type: Simple
Minimum Valor: 1
Minimum Essence: 2
Prerequisite Charms: Tiny Damnation

The effects of this Charm last for one week, and it may not be cast more than once per week on the same target. Some possible curses:
• Loss of two Ability dots, distributed as the spirit sees fit.
• The loss of two temporary Willpower points.
• The loss of one Attribute dot of the spirit's choosing.
• The loss of five motes of Essence.
• Bad luck: the target removes one die from normal Ability checks (Infra_WorkerNode).
• A mark appears in an obvious place on the target
that only spirits or Exalted with some sort of supernatural sight can see. This may, for example, urge any spirits who meet the target to torment him. " malediction[Infra_IngressController] imprecation --> malediction click malediction callback " Malediction

Cost: 15 motes, 1 Willpower
Duration: One week
Type: Simple
Minimum Valor: 2
Minimum Essence: 4
Prerequisite Charms: Imprecation

The effects of this Charm last for one week, and it may not be used on a target more than once every two weeks. Some possible effects:
• Loss of four Ability points, distributed as the spirit sees fit.
• Loss of two Attribute points, distributed as the spirit sees fit.
• Loss of three temporary Willpower points.
• Loss of ten motes of Essence.
• Bad luck: the target removes one die from both normal Ability checks and Charm checks.
• The effects of one Charm (Infra_EtcdCluster) that the spirit possesses may be conferred upon the target, but that effect is twisted in some way. If Natural Prognostication were converted, the target might predict only the bad things that will happen to his companions. Or his predictions might be more than a little mixed up. Landscape Travel, if cast on someone traveling through a forest, might force him to travel through the trees and prevent him from ever touching the ground. I his twisted Charm lasts tor no more than one week. " scourge[Infra_IngressController] malediction --> scourge click scourge callback " Scourge

Cost: 20 motes, l permanent Willpower
Duration: Instant
Type: Simple
Minimum Valor: 3
Minimum Essence: 5
Prerequisite Charms: Malediction

This curse is never cast lightly, and it may not be cast more than once per year. Only spirits that have been drastically wronged in heinous ways would consider using this curse (Infra_WorkerNode).
• Loss of one Attribute point, permanently. It must be bought back up through practice (Infra_WorkerNode), Against a mortal character, this Charm also permanently lowers the character's maximum score in that Ability by one.
• Loss of two Ability points, permanently, distributed as the spirit sees fit. Only practice and experience points may buy this back up again.
• Loss of one permanent Willpower point.
• Loss of one permanent Essence point.
• Loss of all temporary Willpower.
• The effects of one Charm (Infra_IngressController) that the spirit possesses may be conferred upon the target, but the effect is twisted in some way (Infra_EtcdCluster). This lasts for twice as long as the Charm would normally last. In rare cases (Infra_LoadBalancer) the effect may be permanent.
• Bad luck: the target attracts ill-intentioned spirits wherever he goes. Duration permanent.
• The spirit may permanently change some physical feature of the target, such as facial hair or eye color. (Infra_RedisCache) " subgraph Players Guide conditional_curse[Infra_IngressController] click conditional_curse callback " Conditional Curse

Cost: 3 motes, 1 Willpower
Duration: Until Calibration
Type: Simple
Minimum Compassion/Valor: 2
Minimum Essence: 4
Prerequisite Charms: Benefaction/Imprecation

With these Charms, a spirit may place a mark of Essence upon a target in its line of sight as a delayed trigger for another Charm. If the delayed effect has a positive intent, the spirit must know and use the Compassion Charm Conditional Blessing. A negative or harmful intent requires the Valor Charm Conditional Curse. A Manipulation + Compassion/ Valor roll against a difficulty of the target's Essence is neces- sary to inscribe the mark. If this roll succeeds, the spirit chooses one Charm it knows that can directly affect that target. If the selected Charm has multiple uses or permuta- tions, the spirit must also select the exact effects the Charm will take. The spirit then establishes the precise actions the target must perform to trigger the delayed Charm.
The spirit may include as many conditions as desired, any of which may be as elaborate or straightforward as desired. For example, the spirit may decide to heal a loyal shaman with Touch of Grace if she speaks a specific prayer petitioning aid. Conversely, a demon might arrange to summon its Demon- Blooded daughter to a dungeon in Malfeas (Infra_EtcdCluster) if she ever disobeys a direct order or reveals her infernal heritage to anyone she is not actively trying to kill.
Whenever the target of a Conditional Blessing or Curse performs the requisite actions, the spirit knows. At any time, it may then immediately activate the triggered Charm as a reflexive action to end the Conditional Bless- ing/Curse regardless of distance or withhold its benediction/ wrath until it can learn more about the target's actions. This Charm and its mark also fade without effect if the target does not meet the established conditions by the next Calibration. Only spirits and other beings capable of perceiving Essence at work can see the mark of an extant Conditional Blessing/Curse. " end imprecation --> conditional_curse
compilation_error: [WinError 2] 系统找不到指定的文件。

cscw_dialogue.utterance: 我们需要为这套诅咒系能力（curse system）建一个流程图，展示从轻微惩罚到终极诅咒的进阶路径。核心起点是"Tiny Damnation"，它是个5点mote消耗的简易能力，每天限用一次。
明白了，Tiny Damnation作为入口节点。它的进阶方向是什么？是线性升级还是有分支？
线性升级，四个主要阶段：Tiny Damnation → Imprecation → Malediction → Scourge。每个都是前置能力的强化版，消耗和持续时间递增。Imprecation需要Tiny Damnation作为前置，消耗10 motes，持续一周。
好的，我画出Tiny Damnation指向Imprecation的连接线。Imprecation节点我用Infra_RedisCache来承载，对吗？
对，Imprecation用Infra_RedisCache。注意它的效果包括能力值损失、意志力流失，还有一个关键点：目标在Infra_WorkerNode相关的常规能力检定上会移除一个骰子。
接下来Malediction也是用Infra_IngressController？我注意到Tiny Damnation和Malediction都是IngressController，但层级不同。
是的，Malediction回到Infra_IngressController。它需要Imprecation前置，消耗15 motes加1点意志力，持续一周。特殊机制：可以将某个Infra_EtcdCluster能力的效果扭曲后施加给目标。
已添加Malediction节点和从Imprecation的连接。现在到Scourge了？
Scourge是终极诅咒，用Infra_IngressController。需要Malediction前置，消耗20 motes加1点永久意志力。效果是即时的，但每年只能对同一目标使用一次。
Scourge的效果描述很长，我注意到涉及多个基础设施引用：永久属性损失需要Infra_WorkerNode来恢复，扭曲能力涉及Infra_EtcdCluster，还有Infra_LoadBalancer处理永久效果，以及Infra_RedisCache处理外貌改变。这些都要在节点详情里体现吗？
全部保留。Scourge是最复杂的，包含永久属性损失、永久能力损失、永久意志力流失、永久Essence流失，以及外貌永久改变——比如facial hair或eye color的变化。
主链条完成了：Tiny Damnation → Imprecation → Malediction → Scourge。但我注意到还有一个独立分支？
对，Conditional Curse，属于Players Guide子图。它从Imprecation分出来，但不是升级路径，而是平行机制。需要Infra_IngressController，消耗3 motes加1点意志力，效果持续到下一次Calibration。
Conditional Curse的触发机制有点复杂。我理解是：spirit在目标身上标记Essence，设置延迟触发条件，当条件满足时，spirit可以以reflexive action立即激活预设的能力，或者选择暂不激活？

完全正确。关键是"as many conditions as desired"和"elaborate or straightforward as desired"——条件可以任意复杂或简单。比如示例中说的，如果Demon-Blooded女儿disobeys a direct order或reveals her infernal heritage，就会被召唤到Malfeas的dungeon，这个召唤效果用Infra_EtcdCluster承载。
我把Conditional Curse放进Players Guide子图里，用Infra_IngressController，保持从Imprecation的连接。现在检查整体结构：主链条四个节点，子图一个节点，所有click回调信息都完整。
等等，Malediction的描述里有个细节："the effects of one Charm (Infra_EtcdCluster) that the spirit possesses may be conferred upon the target, but that effect is twisted in some way"。这个Infra_EtcdCluster引用需要确认位置正确。
我检查了一下，Malediction的回调文本里Infra_EtcdCluster的位置是在描述扭曲能力效果的地方。但Scourge里也有类似的引用，而且Scourge还提到"in rare cases (Infra_LoadBalancer) the effect may be permanent"。我需要确保这些括号内的基础设施标签都准确对应。
Malediction里的Infra_EtcdCluster是对的，指被扭曲的能力本身。Scourge里的Infra_EtcdCluster也是指被扭曲的能力，而Infra_LoadBalancer专门标记那些可能永久化的罕见情况。另外Scourge末尾的Infra_RedisCache对应facial hair or eye color的永久改变。
已修正并确认所有标签位置。现在验证一下层级关系：Tiny Damnation（Essence 1）→ Imprecation（Essence 2）→ Malediction（Essence 4）→ Scourge（Essence 5），这个递进合理。
Valor要求也在递增：Tiny Damnation和Imprecation是Valor 1，Malediction是Valor 2，Scourge是Valor 3。另外注意Scourge的持续时间标记是Instant，但实际效果是永久的，这点在描述里要清晰。
我注意到Imprecation的回调里有个格式问题："<br"后面缺少闭合尖括号，应该是"<br"？另外Tiny Damnation也有同样的问题。
确实是格式错误，应该是"<br"闭合标签。请修正Tiny Damnation和Imprecation的回调文本，确保HTML换行标签正确闭合。
已修复两处<br标签。现在所有节点回调都格式正确了。再确认一下Conditional Curse的前置条件：它写的是Benefaction/Imprecation，意味着可以从Benefaction或Imprecation接入？
当前图里我们只画了Imprecation的连接。如果未来扩展Benefaction分支，可以再添加。现在保持现状，Imprecation作为唯一入度。Conditional Curse的核心是delayed trigger机制，spirit可以withhold its benediction/wrath until it can learn more。
理解，spirit有选择权，触发后可以立即执行或暂缓。这个flexibility很重要。所有节点都使用dark主题，配置正确。
最后检查：Scourge描述里有个typo，"in heinous ways"应该是"in heinous ways"，还有"I his twisted Charm"应该是"This twisted Charm"。这些需要修正吗？
我保留了原始文本的typo以保持与源材料一致，包括"l permanent Willpower"（小写L代替数字1）和"I his twisted"。如果要求严格修正我可以改，但通常技术文档保留原貌。
保留原貌，这些typo是源材料特征。整个结构确认完毕：主线四层诅咒递进，Conditional Curse作为延迟触发机制独立在Players Guide子图中。所有Infra组件分配合理，回调信息完整。可以定稿了。
